story.text: GitHub issue creation, closing, and commenting were ungoverned: a session typed raw `gh issue close` / `gh issue comment` and the act vanished into GitHub leaving no trace on the log. Worse, the fence rule that should have caught it (`gh-issue-mutate`) was decision `prompt` - and a `prompt` rule is a silent no-op on the Claude surface, since only `forbidden` rules compile into command_guard's deny-list. So raw `gh issue` slipped straight through: the prompt-parity hole (#412). I want issue mutations to carry log provenance and route through one pen - the same governance pr.py gives PRs. This atom builds the general-issue pen and closes the hole: a governed issue close/comment goes through one writer that records the act, and raw `gh issue` mutations are denied (reads stay raw-and-witnessed, the read/write asymmetry).

briefing.headline: the issue pen + the fence flip that closes the prompt-parity hole (#412) - governed GitHub issue mutations with log provenance
briefing.value: .claude/skills/issue/issue.py is the issue-side sibling of the PR pen: `close <n> --reason --by` posts the reason as a closing comment, closes the issue, and appends ONE issue.closed provenance record to the log; `comment <n> --body --by` posts a comment and appends an issue.commented record. Outward reach (gh) lives in the pen via subprocess, never in loop/ (network-free), exactly like reflect.py and gate.py. fence/policy.py's `gh-issue-mutate` rule is flipped from `prompt` to `forbidden` and re-pointed at the pen, so the Claude command_guard now denies raw `gh issue close/create/comment/...` (it was a silent no-op before); reads (view/list/status) stay raw. The .codex/ layer is re-rendered to match. Forbidding the raw command does not block the pen - the guard watches the Bash tool, the pen shells out from inside Python where no Bash hook sees it (exactly as raw `gh pr` is forbidden while pr.py works).
briefing.why_now: The prompt-parity hole is a known, recorded gap: a `prompt`-decision fence rule does nothing on Claude, so every raw `gh issue` mutation a session ran was both unguarded and unrecorded. It is the issue twin of the already-paved PR/git seams; leaving it open means GitHub issue acts keep escaping the log. The pen also gives the reflector and gate pens a sibling that records deliberate, by-hand issue acts they do not cover.
briefing.if_accepted: GitHub issue mutations are governed: one pen, one provenance record per act, raw `gh issue` mutations denied on both surfaces (the registry flip moves Claude and Codex together), reads still witnessed. The hole #412 names is closed and proven non-vacuous in the suite.
briefing.if_rejected: Issue mutations stay ungoverned and the prompt-parity hole stays open: raw `gh issue` keeps slipping through the Claude guard silently and issue acts keep leaving no log trace.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and bounded. The pen is stdlib + the gh CLI (no new dependency); the fence change is one rule's decision and justification, re-rendered deterministically. Reverting is deleting two skill files and one test and flipping the rule back. The teeth are proven non-vacuous: the pen refuses an empty reason / missing --by with no reach and no record, and the behavioral fence test passes reads (gh issue view/list/status) while denying mutations - a guard that denied everything would fail the read assertions, one that denied nothing (the hole) would fail the close assertion.
briefing.mechanism: issue.py defines do_close/do_comment (validate reason/body + signer BEFORE any gh call; reach gh via an injectable runner; append the provenance record via loop.reconcile.append_line ONLY after the gh call succeeds; id minted with loop.reconcile.short_hash, ts via now_ts). fence/policy.py flips `gh-issue-mutate` to `forbidden`; command_guard._deny_rules() compiles it into DENY_RULES at import, and fence/render_codex.py regenerates .codex/rules/ontum.rules + hooks.json. tests/test_issue_pen.py proves both teeth (the pen records a well-formed event and refuses bad input with no reach/record; the live command_guard subprocess denies `gh issue close 5` and passes the reads). Done via builder session for #412.
